q.en: Who finds the two black stones at the beginning of the story?
q.zh: 故事开头，是谁发现了那两块黑石？

options.en: Tala, one of the twin siblings
options.zh: 塔拉，一对双胞胎兄妹中的一个

options.en: Paco
options.zh: 帕科

options.en: Mrs. Yates
options.zh: 耶茨太太

options.en: An Apache warrior according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一名阿帕奇战士（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Tala picks up the two small black stones near the river at the base of Apache Leap Mountain before her brother sees.
explain.zh: 在阿帕奇利普山脚下的河边，塔拉趁弟弟没注意，捡起了两块小黑石。

q.en: What are the black stones actually made of, according to the website the twins find?
q.zh: 根据双胞胎在网站上查到的资料，这些黑石实际上是什么做的？

options.en: Coal
options.zh: 煤炭

options.en: Plastic from a river toy according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 河里玩具上的塑料（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Ordinary beach pebbles
options.zh: 普通的海滩卵石

options.en: Obsidian, a volcanic glass formed when lava cools
options.zh: 黑曜石，一种熔岩冷却后形成的火山玻璃

explain.en: The website shows their stones are obsidian from a volcano, formed inside the earth and cooled.
explain.zh: 网站显示他们的石头是来自火山的黑曜石，形成于地底并冷却而成。

q.en: What legend do the twins read about?
q.zh: 双胞胎读到的是哪一个传说？

options.en: The Apache Tears - warriors jumped off a cliff and women's tears became the black stones
options.zh: “阿帕奇之泪”——战士跳崖身亡，女人们的泪水化作了黑石

options.en: The building of a library according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一座图书馆的建造（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: The founding of Arizona
options.zh: 亚利桑那的建立

options.en: A story about buffaloes
options.zh: 一个关于水牛的故事

explain.en: They read 'Apache Tears': Apache women's tears at their warriors' deaths were embedded in the river's black stones.
explain.zh: 他们读了“阿帕奇之泪”：阿帕奇妇女为死去的战士悲泣，泪水融入了河中的黑石。

q.en: What does 'translucent' mean as used when Tala holds the stones to the sun?
q.zh: 当塔拉把石头举向太阳时，文中的'translucent'是什么意思？

options.en: Dark and solid
options.zh: 漆黑而坚硬

options.en: Made of metal
options.zh: 由金属制成

options.en: Allowing some light to pass through
options.zh: 能让部分光线透过

options.en: Completely clear according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 完全透明（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'translucent' as not clear, but allowing some light to pass through - the stones glowed softly in the sun.
explain.zh: 词汇表将'translucent'定义为虽不透明，但能让部分光线透过——石头在阳光下微微发光。

q.en: How do Tala and Paco see the world differently?
q.zh: 塔拉和帕科看世界的方式有何不同？

options.en: Tala sees it like a scientist (questions/facts); Paco sees it as magical/poetic
options.zh: 塔拉像科学家般看待（提问/事实）；帕科视之为神奇/诗意

options.en: Paco is a scientist; Tala writes poems according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 帕科是科学家；塔拉写诗（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: They see it exactly the same
options.zh: 他们看世界的方式完全一样

options.en: Tala likes sports; Paco likes school
options.zh: 塔拉喜欢运动；帕科喜欢上学

explain.en: Tala views the world as a scientist full of questions, while Paco sees it as a magical place of poems waiting to be written.
explain.zh: 塔拉像科学家一样把世界看成充满问题，而帕科把它看作等待书写的神奇诗篇。

q.en: What did Mrs. Yates ask the twins to do as a gift to her?
q.zh: 耶茨太太让双胞胎把她当礼物，去做什么事？

options.en: Stop going to the river
options.zh: 不再去河边

options.en: Give the stones to a museum according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 把石头捐给博物馆（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Sell the stones
options.zh: 把石头卖掉

options.en: Research the stones together without arguing
options.zh: 一起研究石头，并且不吵架

explain.en: Mrs. Yates asks them to do an experiment as a gift: research the stones without arguing, to learn to work together.
explain.zh: 耶茨太太要求他们送她一份礼物：不吵架地一起研究石头，学会合作。

q.en: The twins' search combined two ideas. What did they learn?
q.zh: 双胞胎的搜索结合了两种想法。他们得出了什么结论？

options.en: They are made of gold
options.zh: 它们是金子做的

options.en: The stones are both volcanic obsidian AND considered good-luck 'Apache Tears'
options.zh: 这些石头既是火山黑曜石，也被视为带来好运的“阿帕奇之泪”

options.en: They are from the ocean according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 它们来自海洋（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: They are fake
options.zh: 它们是假的

explain.en: Paco and Tala realized both were right: the stones are volcanic obsidian and also the good-luck Apache Tears.
explain.zh: 帕科和塔拉意识到两人都对：石头既是火山黑曜石，也是带来好运的“阿帕奇之泪”。

q.en: Why does the author include the mother's grief over her husband's death?
q.zh: 作者为什么要写母亲对亡夫的悲伤？

options.en: To show she dislikes stones according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了表现她讨厌石头（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To explain why the twins' fighting hurts her and why they learn to be a family again
options.zh: 为了说明双胞胎的争吵如何伤她的心，以及他们如何重新学会成为一家人

options.en: To teach geology
options.zh: 为了讲授地质学

options.en: To describe a funeral
options.zh: 为了描写一场葬礼

explain.en: The mother's grief shows the twins' arguing adds to her pain, motivating their later change and family healing.
explain.zh: 母亲的悲伤表明，双胞胎的争吵加深了她的痛楚，也推动了他们后来的转变与家庭的和解。

q.en: What do Tala and Paco do with the stones at the end?
q.zh: 结尾处，塔拉和帕科怎样处置了这些石头？

options.en: Sell them to a jeweler
options.zh: 卖给珠宝匠

options.en: Keep one each forever
options.zh: 每人永远留一块

options.en: Give them to their mother, who has them made into a necklace
options.zh: 把石头送给母亲，母亲把它们做成了一条项链

options.en: Throw them back in the river according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 把它们扔回河里（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The twins give the stones to their mother; she sleeps with them and later has them set into a necklace she never removes.
explain.zh: 双胞胎把石头送给母亲；她枕着石头入睡，后来请人把它们镶成一条从不摘下的项链。

q.en: What is the main lesson the twins learn?
q.zh: 双胞胎学到的主要教训是什么？

options.en: That magic is real
options.zh: 魔法真实存在

options.en: That family and getting along matter more than objects
options.zh: 家人之情与和睦相处，比物件更重要

options.en: That science is always wrong according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 科学总是错的（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: That rocks are worthless
options.zh: 石头毫无价值

explain.en: They understand their arguing only adds to their mother's grief and that they must live in harmony as a family.
explain.zh: 他们明白争吵只会加重母亲的悲伤，必须作为一家人和谐相处。

prompt.en: If you found a special stone that was both a science mystery and part of a legend, what would you name it and what story would you tell about it?
prompt.zh: 如果你找到一块既是科学之谜、又属于某个传说的特殊石头，你会给它起什么名字，又会讲述怎样的故事？

en: Combine one real fact about rocks with one imaginative detail.
Think about why the stone would be meaningful to your family.
zh: 把一个关于石头的真实事实与一个想象中的细节结合起来。
想想这块石头对你的家庭为何意义非凡。
